violations.text: Corn was held at 47 degrees on refrigerator F. Technician on-site stated there was inadequate air flow inside unit. Staff relocated some items to increase air flow during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2026
Establishment is using non-continuous cooking for chicken. PIC to submit written plan to RIDOH for approval. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025
Refrigerator A was not in good repair. Technician was on-site at the time of this inspection, unit has been taken out of use. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2026

Molluscan Shellfish tags are not maintained for 90 days. Molluscan shellfish tags or labels are required to be maintained for 90 calendar days from the date the container is emptied. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025
Large ice cubes at the bar were observed in a bin with frost from the inside edges of the freezer. Food must be protected from contamination. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the bar dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025

Potatoes, which were prepared in advance (yesterday), were not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025

Shell eggs observed held at 57-67 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025
Establishment uses non-continuous cooking for chicken. A written plan must be submitted and approved by RIDOH. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Potatoes were placed hot in the refrigerator in a plastic container greater than four inches deep, and covered with plastic wrap. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025

Refrigerator A was not in good repair, not holding food at 41 degrees F or below. Ambient temperature readings were 46 and 51. Refrigerator A is not to be used to store any foods requiring refrigeration until it has been serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the kitchen dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the bar dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonia sanitizing solution for the kitchen 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:bar blender baseice machineplate storage unitsrefrigeratorsprep unitscold drawersdrawer unit on cook linetables/cold drawer units beneath grillskitchen shelves including the undersidescondiment bins on bottom shelfThe wall-mounted fan had an accumulation of dust. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025
The oil recycling receptacle has an accumulation of grease and grime.Receptacles are to be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2026
The oil recycling and trash areas have an accumulation of leaf litter. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2026
Four lights beneath the hood were nonfunctional during inspection. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2026

The ansul system pipes beneath the hood have an accumulation of dust and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2026

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2026

Ceiling tiles above refrigerators are in poor repair, warped from previous water damage.A ceiling tile above the cooking line has been pushed aside for ceiling repair work. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the slicer has an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/30/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the clean linen area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 05/30/2024

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety licensed with RIDOH Center for food protection. Staff are enrolled in class and must register with RIDOH upon completion. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

The following foods were observed held at 45-61 degrees Fahrenheit: butternut, sauces, soups, cheeses, turkey, assorted sandwiches, spreads, juice, and cooked vegetables. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/24/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per staff, several foods were placed hot into sliding door refrigerator M with lids or covered in plastic wrap. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the cooking area. Staff placed on clean sanitized tray. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

One refrigerator (M) was not in good repair, not holding food at 41 degrees F or below. Unit taken out of service for repair; new unit obtained during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/24/2024
Bowl for the large stand mixer was not in good repair, observed with gouges and uneven (not smooth) welds. New Violation. Correct By: 05/24/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -area under flattop grill -grill knobs -small stand mixer -bread refrigerator, including door tracks Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024
Pooled water of unknown origin was observed in the liquor/beverage closet. New Violation. Correct By: 05/24/2024

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety registered with RIDOH Center for Food Protection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer, pans) were observed with an accumulation of food, debris, and water. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Staff re-cleaned and sanitized slicer during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Staff were observed placing washed strawberries into an unclean and un-sanitized container Staff informed, re-washed strawberries and placed in clean container. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Scrambled eggs, which were prepared in advance, were not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Beef and shrimp were observed held at 48- and 47- degrees Fahrenheit in refrigerator J. Soup cold-holding on ice was observed at 69- degrees Fahrenheit. Sliced tomatoes in the salad prep area were observed at 45 degrees. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were moved to walk-in freezer to rapidly chill. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Eggs were placed hot in the walk-in refrigerator in plastic-wrapped containers, and hot soup was placed in a plastic container greater than 4 inches deep in the shallow ice on the cook line used for cold holding. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the the cooking area. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: slicer plate racks area beneath flattop grill bread refrigerator including door tracks Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety that is registered and licensed with RIDOH Center for Food Protection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided guidance during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer, plates, serving plates, large salad bowl, mixers, blender, small ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. Hair was observed in the ice bin at the soda station. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Hash browns, which were prepared in advance, were not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Several foods that were recently prepared were observed held between 45- and 56 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Items were moved to walk-in to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
Diced onion and stock were observed held past 7 days. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Several working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Bottles were labeled during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Cooked pasta was placed in walk-in in containers greater than four inches deep. Hash browns prepared earlier in morning were observed at 50 degrees. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Plates observed stored in plate racks with debris, food debris, and chips of glass and ceramic. Portion cups and portion cup lids observed stored in bins with debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Single service items observed stored on surfaces that were not clean and sanitized in the kitchen. Boxes of single service items were observed stored on the floor in the upstairs storage area. Single service items must be stored in a clean location not subject to dust, splash or other contamination and at least 6 inches off the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Plastic wrap is used to wrap shelving in the dish wash area; foil is used to line several shelves. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024
The following were observed in poor repair: -whisk to small mixer -robot coupe base -stand mixer (peeling paint) -plastic coated strainer New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:-shelving throughout the facility-plate racks-bin of miscellaneous tools at dish wash area-working containers of dry ingredients-mixers-lights under hoods-under grill top-shelves in Reach-in E-plate well edges-multiple drawers-portion cup bins-knife rack-prep unit-lock latch on reach-ins-shelving at bar refrigerators.Old rodent droppings were observed in the upstairs storage area. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024
Leaks were observed at the 3-bay sink and mop sink faucet. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

The floors throughout the kitchen (especially at wall/floor junctures) and the wall behind the grill line have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024
Two ice buckets are not stored inverted. After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils must be air dried. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/18/2024

One ice bucket observed stored on an unclean surface on top of the ice machine. Equipment and utensils must be stored in a clean, dry location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/18/2024
The door and gasket on the large ice machine was not in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the unused old bread refrigerator, prep unit, table beneath grill, and grill have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 01/18/2024
The gasket on the walk in freezer is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

Food contact surfaces of the ice machines were observed with an accumulation of soil residue and biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machines were taken out of service. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

Food contact surfaces of numerous plate in the clean plate racks were observed with an accumulation of black debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

Chicken, squash, chowder, scampi, onion dip, sauces, chicken noodle soup observed held at 43-47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023
Date marking exceeding 7 days was observed on scampi and sauce. Disposed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken was placed hot in the refrigerator in covered plastic container greater than four inches deep. Assorted items from the hot line last night were covered and double panned, then placed in the refrigerator. New Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

Two ice buckets observed stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023
One ice bucket observed not stored inverted. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the upstairs dry storage area. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

The slicer was not in good repair, with the silicon seal between ragged in some places, pieces missing in others, and there is exposed hardware where caps are missing. Slicer must be removed, refurbished or replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023
The table top mixer was not in good repair, with the coating missing on the attachment port, which is also cracked. New Violation. Correct By: 01/03/2024
Gaskets on walk-in freezer were observed in poor repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023
The door and gaskets of the large ice machine is not in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonia sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:plate racksbread refrigerator including door handlescooking line wellsslicermixersareas beneath the grill, flattop grill, and fryer splash guards Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

The walk in fan guard covers, has an accumulation of soil residue.The light bulb cover and cage of the burned out lightbulb beneath the hood had an accumulation of oil and grease.A small amount of old rodent droppings observed in the upstairs storage area. The physical shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023
One light bulb beneath the hood was nonfunctional at time of inspection. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/03/2024
No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

The dishwasher failed to wash his/her hands between loading soiled and unloading clean dishes from the dishwasher. Food employees must clean their hands after handling soiled equipment or utensils. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

Food contact surfaces of 2 large ice machines had an accumulation of biofilm and soil residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machines were taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher at the bar was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Establishment must utilize 3-bay sink to sanitize equipment until dish machine is properly serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer, mixers, clean equipment on clean storage rack) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

Fish, shrimp, scallops, cut tomatoes, cut lettuce, sliced turkey were observed held between 44-61 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Fish, shrimp, scallops, tomatoes, and lettuce were disposed of; turkey was moved to another unit for cold hold during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

Ham, onion dip, and soup observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023
Working containers (spray bottles) used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

Per employee, unpasteurized raw egg whites are used as an ingredient in one cocktail. Pasteurized egg whites are required when using raw egg whites as an ingredient. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

Thermometers are lacking in the several refrigeration units. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023

Working containers of flour, rice, and other grains were not labeled with the common name of the food. Food is stored in containers with old labels from foods previously stored in them. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings observed in the upstairs dry storage area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies.Ecolab pest control service monthly. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023
Food observed stored on the floor in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

The ice scoops are stored on unclean surfaces (tops of the ice machines) between uses. Scoops for dry ingredients are stored in unclean holders on bins.During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023
Water observed in large mixing bowl on rack, and in ice buckets.After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils must be air dried. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean on storage shelves, storack rack, and cooking line wells.Single service items must be stored in a clean dry location, where they are not subject to dust, splash, or other contamination, and at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023
The whisk to the small mixer was missing several wires; several plates were observed chipped. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink, and the chlorine sanitizer at the bar dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: cook line prep unit several reach-in refrigerators fan guards of walk-ins blue condiment and portion cup bins storage shelvesbread storage refrigerator including door handlescooking line wellsslicermixerprep unitareas beneath grill, flattop grill, 2 burner unit, and stove.Shelves in the upstairs dry storage area have a small accumulation of rodent droppings. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023
Dumpster area has accumulation of litter, leaf litter, and unused items. Dumpster areas must be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023
One light bulb under hood was non-functional at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the cooking area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023
